A chef named Ignacio in Piedras Negras, Mexico is said to have lent his nickname to this dish using tortilla chips
This type of internal combustion engine was named for a German guy with the first name of Rudolf
In 4-letter figure skating jumps, the Axel is from a skater's first name & this one was originally a last name
From the name of an Old Testament patriarch, this adjective refers collectively to Judaism, Christianity & Islam
Named for a Paris professor of medicine, this syndrome causes numbness due to cold or stress
Lady Caroline Lamb, once the lover of this poet, caricatured him as Lord Glenarvon in an 1816 novel
What Carl Sandburg was to Chicago, Amiri Baraka was to this largest New Jersey city with poems like "Lullaby of Avon Ave."
Best known for writing about Lady Liberty, she penned "Venus of the Louvre" about a different statue
In the 1987 film "Barfly", this L.A. poet who inspired the film fittingly plays a patron in a bar
In 2020 Juan Cole put out a new English version of the "Rubáiyát of" this Persian poet, most famous in a 19th c. translation
Fleur-de-lis can refer to a lily as well as to this flower whose name also has 4 letters
A contemporary of Linnaeus, German botanist Traugott Gerber has a variety of this flower named in his honor

That's Rory McIlroy about to chip his ball out of these flowering bushes at the 2018 Masters

[IMAGE CLUE]
Touch-me-not is another name for a variety of this flower, & please don't keep us waiting

[IMAGE CLUE]
Carlsbad, California is famous for its fields of these flowers of the buttercup family that carpet the hills in spring
In Korea, the equivalent of the fairy tale opener "Once upon a time" is "Back when" these big cats "used to smoke"
The city of Oral in this country was founded around 1620 by the Cossacks, mighty horsemen with a name related to the country's
Rudyard Kipling's poem has inspired some to refer to the Irrawaddy River as "the road to" this city
In April 1975 victorious Vietnamese forces announced that this city had been renamed after Ho Chi Minh
In 1971 George Harrison & Ravi Shankar hosted the "Concert for" this country, which served as a benefit for its people

categories.name: THE 18th CENTURY WORLD WITH JAMIE FRASER

questions.clue:
[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sam Heughan presents the clue.) Reaching continental America, we become involved with this Iroquois tribe that sided with the British; I believe the production did justice to the famous hairstyles

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sam Heughan presents the clue.) Clare & Jamie travel to France to try to influence the Jacobites, & encounter King Louis XV at this palace that he made once again the seat of government

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sam Heughan presents the clue.) When we crossed the Atlantic, who but our friend & Jamie's admirer, Lord John Grey, should be governor of this island of bondage & mystery when its capital was still called Spanish Town

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sam Heughan presents the clue.) Jamie gets to meet Scottish heroine Flora MacDonald, famous for helping Prince Charlie make a daring escape, as heard in the "Outlander" theme song, "over the sea to" this isle

[IMAGE CLUE]
(Sam Heughan presents the clue.) In pre-Revolution America Jamie & Clare are caught up in a rebellion in this coastal state where Governor Tryon angered the people by building a palace at New Bern

final.clue: In his memoir, he referenced Alfred Hitchcock's "The Birds" while writing about a near-disaster
